name: threejs-animation
description: Three.js animation - keyframe animation, skeletal animation, morph targets, animation mixing. Use when animating objects, playing GLTF animations, creating procedural motion, or blending animations.
capabilities: Create and play keyframe animations using AnimationClip and AnimationMixer
Load and control skeletal animations from GLTF models
Animate morph targets for smooth mesh shape transitions
Blend multiple animations together with weighted transitions
Implement procedural animation patterns like springs and smooth damping
Control animation timing, loops, fades, and playback states
author: cloudai-x
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. This is a documentation-only skill containing JavaScript code examples. No executable code, shell commands, network requests, filesystem operations, or cryptographic operations exist. The scanner misidentified JavaScript template literals as shell backticks and animation properties as cryptographic algorithms.
valueStatement: Adding smooth, professional animations to 3D web applications is complex. This skill provides comprehensive documentation and code examples for Three.js animation features including keyframes, skeletal rigs, morph targets, and animation blending.
limitations: Does not provide executable code - only documentation and examples
Does not include pre-made animation assets or GLTF models
Does not provide runtime animation rendering or visualization

useCases.title: Game Developer - Character Animation
useCases.description: Implement character locomotion systems by loading GLTF animations and blending idle, walk, and run states based on player input.

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers adding polish to web experiences
useCases.title: Frontend Developer - UI Transitions
useCases.description: Create smooth camera movements and object animations for product showcases, data visualizations, and interactive landing pages.

useCases.targetUser: Technical artists working on animation tools
useCases.title: Technical Artist - Animation Pipelines
useCases.description: Programmatically generate animation clips, optimize keyframe data, and create procedural motion for procedural character generation systems.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Animation Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a simple Three.js animation that rotates and moves a mesh using requestAnimationFrame and the THREE.Clock timing system.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with basic procedural animation

promptTemplates.title: Loading GLTF Animations
promptTemplates.prompt: Load a GLTF model and play its walk animation. Show how to create an AnimationMixer, find specific clips by name, and update the mixer in the animation loop.

promptTemplates.title: Animation Blending
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an animation system that smoothly blends between idle, walking, and running animations based on character speed. Include fade-in, fade-out, and crossfade between actions.

promptTemplates.title: Procedural Motion
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a spring physics system and smooth damp function for procedural object movement. Show how to animate bones programmatically and blend procedural motion with keyframe clips.

bestPractices: Always call mixer.update(delta) in your animation loop - forgotten updates are the most common animation bug.
Use clip.optimize() to remove redundant keyframes and reduce memory usage for complex animations.
Pause mixer updates for off-screen objects to maintain performance in large scenes.
antiPatterns: Creating a new AnimationMixer for every animated object - share clips and pool mixers instead.
Playing multiple actions without managing weights - always set effective weights for blended animations.
Forgetting to call mixer.stopAllAction() before switching animation states, causing conflicts.

faq.question: What is the difference between AnimationClip and AnimationAction?
faq.answer: AnimationClip contains the raw keyframe data. AnimationAction controls playback of a clip on a mixer - you can play, pause, fade, and weight each action independently.

faq.question: How do I make an animation play only once and stop?
faq.answer: Set action.loop = THREE.LoopOnce and action.clampWhenFinished = true. The animation will hold its final frame when complete.

faq.question: Can I animate multiple models with the same animation?
faq.answer: Yes, share the AnimationClip across multiple AnimationMixers. Each mixer maintains its own playback state.

faq.question: How do I control animation playback speed?
faq.answer: Set action.timeScale. Positive values play forward, negative values play backward. Use 0.5 for half speed, 2 for double speed.

faq.question: What is the difference between normal and additive blending?
faq.answer: Normal blending replaces the pose. Additive blending adds to the base pose. Additive is useful for breathing, aiming offsets, and facial animations layered over locomotion.

faq.question: How do I find and play a specific animation from a GLTF file?
faq.answer: Access gltf.animations array, use THREE.AnimationClip.findByName(clips, 'AnimationName') to locate the clip, then create and play an action for it.
